_comment: Queue for the Protein of the Week harness. research.py pops the first entry of 'upcoming' unless a protein is passed on the CLI. Move entries to 'published' (with number + date) once an issue ships. Add your own; 'hint' is optional seed context passed to the researchers.

upcoming.hint: Banting & Best (Toronto, 1921); Sanger's sequencing (1951, first protein sequenced); first recombinant-DNA drug, Humulin (Genentech / Eli Lilly, 1982).
From Thermus aquaticus, a Yellowstone hot-spring bacterium (Brock, 1969); made PCR practical; Kary Mullis, 1993 Nobel.
Max Perutz's decades-long X-ray structure (1959, Nobel 1962); oxygen transport; sickle-cell as the first 'molecular disease' (Pauling, 1949).
Photinus pyralis bioluminescence; ATP-dependent light; ubiquitous reporter and ATP assay.
CRISPR-associated nuclease; Streptococcus pyogenes; Doudna & Charpentier, 2012; 2020 Nobel; genome editing.
One of the first enzymes named (Schwann, 1836); crystallized by Northrop (1930s, Nobel 1946); digestion; acid protease.
